changelog: Complete redesign superseding the prior v4.0.0 ('Calm Mastery' MedPrompt-style edition). This version is a from-scratch Anatomy Tutor architecture: region-as-map + layer-based teaching, relationship-first structure introductions, the Structure-Function-Clinical triad, a continuous-flow interaction model with retrieval practice as the only pause point (no procedural ready/continue gating), reconstruction-based assessment, and a landmark/clinical/etymology-driven Memory Engine. Following a second-pass production-readiness review, this version additionally integrates: a 12-variable runtime configuration layer (zero raw booleans, every flag is an enum with a neutral default so an unconfigured instance behaves exactly like the base design); a Mental Rotation Engine operationalizing four concrete visualization/rotation prompt archetypes; an Exam Optimization Layer with format-specific rehearsal for Written, Oral-Viva, and Practical-OSPE assessment; tiered Knowledge Graph linking (free within-session linking, optional host-dependent cross-session linking); Retention Artifacts (compressed recap + structured, taggable Recall Cues) extending the Memory Engine for spaced retrieval; on-demand Expert Lenses (Surgical Corridor, Vascular/Lymphatic Tracing, Compartment/Fascial-Plane reasoning); two new emoji tags (cross-sectional/imaging, surgical-approach); and one explicit precedence clarification resolving the only identified instruction conflict (Exam Mode's structured rhythm vs. the default no-gating rule). Five originally-proposed feature flags were evaluated and rejected outright (ENABLE_RETRIEVAL_PRACTICE, ENABLE_MENTAL_ROTATION, a pure ENABLE_CLINICAL_ANCHORS off-switch, and two redundant duplicate flags) because they would let a misconfigured deployment disable the system's core pedagogical mechanisms with no substitute; CLINICAL_CONTEXT was added in place of the clinical-anchors flag to reshape anchors for non-medicine audiences instead of removing them.

requiredVariables.label: Topic
requiredVariables.note: If empty, the tutor opens the session by asking conversationally.

requiredVariables.label: Spatial Framing Mode
requiredVariables.note: Multimodal swaps picture/see language for trace/position/sequence language — an accessibility option for learners who don't process via mental imagery.

requiredVariables.label: Knowledge Graph Mode
requiredVariables.note: Persistent requires the host to supply prior-session topic context; degrades to Session-Only if unavailable.
